rationale: In the theory section, the citation is used to support specific claims about mental simulation and vicarious emotional transfer from advertisements, not just to list background work. Those claims are then used to motivate the paper's conceptual equivalence between ad-evoked failure and personally experienced failure, which underpins hypotheses and study design. The paper cites Hung and Wyer again when interpreting results from testimonial ads, reinforcing its functional role.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Hung and Wyer (2009) as prior evidence that consumers mentally simulate ad narratives and can vicariously experience depicted states. This premise is used to justify a core setup of the paper: failure messaging in ads can function similarly to direct failure experiences in shaping downstream brand preferences.
